config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap jadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu ditempatkan di atas semuanya, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama mayoritas. Saya melihat kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika individu dilindungi, bukan dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society’s interests should automatically outrank individual rights; in the United States, that kind of thinking has too often been used to excuse abuses of power. I do think there are real cases where public safety or the common good justify limits on individual freedom, but those limits have to be narrow, lawful, and tied to basic rights. If the majority can override the individual whenever it wants, then minority rights become fragile and freedom turns into a popularity contest. To me, the better principle is that society should serve people, not absorb them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kecil, mengabaikan minoritas, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang tidak transparan. Bagi saya, kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar agar negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi warganya satu per satu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have blanket priority over the individual, because in the United States that principle has repeatedly been used to justify overreach and abuse. The strongest version of a society is not one that can sacrifice people whenever it claims a common good, but one that can protect rights while still making narrow, justified exceptions for public safety. I also disagree with the idea that the majority’s interests should carry the final word, because minority rights are exactly what get crushed when “society first” becomes a slogan instead of a carefully limited policy. To me, the burden should always be on society to prove that any restriction on individual rights is truly necessary, not just convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diutamakan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menutup ketidakadilan, terutama saat suara minoritas dianggap mengganggu atau saat keputusan dibuat tanpa transparansi. Bagi saya, yang benar adalah kebalikannya: masyarakat harus dibangun untuk melindungi hak dasar setiap orang, lalu baru membatasi hak itu kalau memang ada alasan yang sangat jelas, sempit, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, slogan “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alat pembenaran bagi kekuasaan mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that phrase too often becomes a blank check for government overreach or majority pressure, and history shows how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse discrimination or silence unpopular groups. I do think there are limited cases where public safety justifies temporary restrictions, but the burden must be extremely high and the rights involved must remain the default starting point. A society is strongest when it protects individuals first and only limits them when it can prove the need is real, narrow, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
